pattern: Merge rows one at a time by combining the current k-smallest sums with the next row's sorted values using a min-heap, keeping only the k smallest candidates.
hints: Reduce the problem row by row: find the k smallest sums picking one element from each of the first i rows, then extend to row i+1.

When combining a sorted list of up to k sums with a sorted row of n values, a min-heap initialized with (sum + row[0]) for each existing sum and expanded via (sum + row[j+1]) explores candidates in order.
Because k is at most 200 and each row has at most 40 elements, the heap-based merge over m rows runs in manageable time.

edge_inputs.description: single-row matrix — kth smallest is just the kth element
single-column matrix — sum is fixed, only 1 combination
k=1 is always the minimum possible sum (first column of each row)
k=5 requires careful enumeration of combinations
k equals maximum possible — need the largest valid sum

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = size
    n = 40
    mat = []
    for _ in range(m):
        row = sorted(rng.randint(1, 5000) for _ in range(n))
        mat.append(row)
    k = 200
    return [mat, k]
